repo_prior_art.cite: stat_sa_cate_pointwise_v1 studies non-private localized DR CATE, not a central-DP randomized subexperiment.

published_prior_art.cite: CaiChakrabortyVuursteen2024OptimalFederated gives related pointwise private regression bounds, but in distributed/federated DP rather than this central-DP CATE embedding.
KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE supplies the non-private benchmark; the claimed boundary is routine rate algebra once the oracle-private rate is established.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_sa_cate_pointwise_v1 is non-private and does not formulate a DP causal minimax frontier.

published_prior_art.cite: KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE is non-private; CaiChakrabortyVuursteen2024OptimalFederated is federated regression; SchroderMelnychukFeuerriegel2025DPCATE and NiuNoriQuistorffEtAl2022DPHeterogeneous give DP CATE procedures without a pointwise minimax characterization.

novelty_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship-aspirant gap names comparator bibkeys but provides no section, equation, page, or quoted open-problem anchor for the asserted pointwise-minimax gap.

soundness_flags.label: Proposition 1 / prop:privacy-free-reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: Its asymptotic branches require a privacy sequence epsilon_n (and its quantification relative to epsilon_star,n); epsilon is currently only a scalar parameter.

soundness_flags.label: Oracle randomized submodel / ass:oracle-response
soundness_flags.one_line: The submodel is called causal and uses tau_P as a potential-outcome CATE, but it omits consistency and exchangeability; add them or explicitly redefine this theorem as an observed-regression submodel.

soundness_flags.label: ass:oracle-response
soundness_flags.one_line: Tsybakov2009Nonparametric supports ordinary nonparametric regression, not the named randomized causal embedding E[Y|A=a,X=x]=a tau_P(x); retag this deliberately constructed lower-bound submodel as novel and justify containment.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add exact literature anchors for Kennedy et al. (Theorem 1 and Section 4/B.8), Cai et al. (pointwise-rate theorem), and the two DP-CATE papers’ theorem/experiment sections that show the absence of a pointwise minimax result.
State epsilon=epsilon_n and delta=delta_n, give the uniform quantifiers for Theorem 1, and formulate the phase transition with epsilon_n/epsilon_star,n to make both asymptotic branches precise.

Make the oracle embedding explicitly causal by adding consistency and randomized exchangeability, or recast it as a regression reduction and prove the transfer to the causal class; then state the exact central-DP lower-bound route and its delta dependence.

Promote the full frontier OEQ into paired upper- and lower-bound conjectures once a concrete privatized local-HOIF release and causal fuzzy/tracing family are specified, including any third-regime candidate.

recommended_next_step: Revise the oracle submodel and phase quantifiers, add literature-location anchors, then continue to Stage 0 on the retained central-DP observational CATE frontier.
